\begin{document}

\title{3\textdegree{} Longitudinal Periodicity in the UNESCO World Heritage Corpus:\\
Enrichment in Domed Monuments and Harmonic Cluster Asymmetry}

\author{Seth Tenenbaum\thanks{ORCID: \href{https://orcid.org/0009-0008-5797-2498}{0009-0008-5797-2498}.
Correspondence: seth@fourthtemple.com}\\
Independent Scholar, USA}

\maketitle

\abstract{
Domed and spherical UNESCO World Heritage monuments ($N=\NcircTotal$) are
significantly concentrated near a pre-specified 3\textdegree{} longitudinal
grid anchored to Mount Gerizim (35.269\textdegree{}E).
Three primary tests survive Holm correction ($k=\BonfK$):
a dome von~Mises mixture ($p_{\mathrm{adj}}=\pHolmTestOne$, Test~1),
Spearman rank correlation between harmonic node deviation and cluster size
($p_{\mathrm{adj}}=\pHolmTestTwo$, Test~2),
and a phase-rotation null on the full-corpus mean harmonic deviation
($p_{\mathrm{adj}}=\pHolmTestThree$, Test~3).
Both the anchor and period are stated hypotheses, not conclusions of the analysis.
The pipeline is fully reproducible.
}

\bigskip

\noindent\textbf{Keywords:}
longitude periodicity, spatial statistics, Levant, UNESCO World Heritage,
3-degree harmonics, domed architecture, circular statistics,
monument distribution, Mount Gerizim

\bigskip
